patterns.name: Missing Type Prefix
patterns.template: Commit message must start with a valid type prefix: feat, fix, chore, docs, style, refactor, perf, test, build, ci

patterns.name: Missing Colon After Type
patterns.template: Type (and optional scope) must be followed by `: ` — e.g., `feat: description` or `feat(scope): description`

patterns.name: Missing Space After Colon
patterns.template: Add a space after the colon: `{type}:description` → `{type}: description`

patterns.name: Subject Too Short
patterns.template: Subject is too short ({length} chars). Provide a meaningful description of the change (10+ chars recommended).

example.good: fix(auth): correct typo in OAuth2 callback URL

patterns.name: Subject Too Long
patterns.template: Subject line exceeds 72 characters ({length} chars). Move details to the commit body.

example.bad: feat(auth): add comprehensive OAuth2 authentication flow with Google, GitHub, and Microsoft providers including PKCE support
example.good: feat(auth): add multi-provider OAuth2 with PKCE

patterns.name: Subject Ends With Period
patterns.template: Subject should not end with a period.

patterns.name: Subject Starts With Uppercase
patterns.template: Consider starting the subject with a lowercase letter for consistency.

patterns.name: Invalid Conventional Type
patterns.template: '{invalidType}' is not a valid Conventional Commits type. Use one of: feat, fix, chore, docs, style, refactor, perf, test, build, ci

patterns.name: Empty Scope Parentheses
patterns.template: Empty scope parentheses. Either add a scope or remove the parentheses.

patterns.name: Breaking Change Without Body
patterns.template: Breaking changes (!) should include a body with BREAKING CHANGE footer explaining what changed and how to migrate.

patterns.name: WIP Commit Message
patterns.template: WIP commits should be squashed before merging. Use `git rebase -i` to clean up history.

example.good: feat(auth): add login form with validation
